name: entropykit
description: Syntax highlighting + debugger for EntropyKit (.etpy) - a red-team-first compiled language that emits position-independent Windows x86-64 shellcode AND Cobalt Strike Beacon Object Files. F5 on a .etpy auto-detects BOF vs shellcode, recompiles, and steps the execution arrow through source.

program.description: Path to a .etpy source (recommended) OR a compiled .bin/.x64.o/.obj/.coff. When pointed at a .etpy the adapter sniffs BOF (`fn go(...)`) vs shellcode (`fn main(...)`) from the source, rebuilds, then debugs the freshly-built artifact. When pointed at a compiled artifact the adapter loads it as-is unless `build: true` is set. `${file}` (the active editor's path) is the recommended value: F5 always traces whatever .etpy is open.

dbg.description: Override path to the .dbg source map. Defaults to <artifact>.dbg. Rarely needed - every `entc compile --debug` writes one.

stopOnEntry.description: Pause at the first .etpy statement instead of running to a user-set breakpoint. Highly recommended - it confirms the right file was actually launched.

build.description: Force a rebuild before debugging. Implicit (and unavoidable) when `program` is a .etpy source: the adapter always rebuilds source-mode launches so the running artifact matches the editor. Only honoured for .bin/.x64.o/.obj/.coff `program` shapes.

source.description: Override the .etpy source path when `program` is a compiled artifact. Defaults to the .etpy that sits next to `program`. Ignored when `program` is already a .etpy.

entc.description: Path to the `entc` compiler binary. If unset, the adapter walks up from the source file looking for `target/release/entc.exe` then `target/debug/entc.exe`.

args.description: Arguments handed to a BOF's `go(args, len)`. Mirrors the `bof-runner` CLI: pairs of flag + value, packed in Aggressor's `bof_pack` order. Flags: `--zarg STR` (zstring), `--iarg N` (big-endian int), `--sarg N` (big-endian short), `--barg @path` (binary blob), `--args RAW` (raw bytes, no length prefix). Ignored when the source compiles to shellcode.

configurationSnippets.label: EntropyKit: Trace current .etpy (auto-detect)
configurationSnippets.description: F5 the active .etpy. The adapter auto-detects BOF vs shellcode from the source (`fn go(...)` => BOF, `fn main(...)` => shellcode), rebuilds, and traces. The recommended config for almost every workflow.

configurationSnippets.label: EntropyKit: Trace current .etpy (BOF with args)
configurationSnippets.description: Same auto-detect flow, but with sample BOF args. Edit `args` to match your `go(args, len)` consumer.

configurationSnippets.label: EntropyKit: Run pre-built .bin (no rebuild)
configurationSnippets.description: Run an already-compiled .bin shellcode artifact. Skip the rebuild - useful when investigating an artifact you didn't produce locally. Artifacts default to <source-dir>/bin/.

configurationSnippets.label: EntropyKit: Run pre-built .x64.o BOF (no rebuild)
configurationSnippets.description: Run an already-compiled .x64.o BOF artifact. Skip the rebuild - useful when investigating an artifact you didn't produce locally. Artifacts default to <source-dir>/bin/.
